title: Clathrospermum heudelotii
taxonomy: Clathrospermum heudelotii is a flowering plant species in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Clathrospermum. The classification of Clathrospermum heudelotii is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Magnoliids, order - Magnoliales, family - Annonaceae, genus - Clathrospermum, species - C. heudelotii. Further, it is native to tropical Africa. The exact classification may vary based on the source and the most recent taxonomic updates.

intro: Clathrospermum heudelotii is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as a tropical vine or climber found in West and Central Africa. The leaves and stem bark of Clathrospermum heudelotii have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and pain relief. Bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Clathrospermum heudelotii is typically used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as traditional remedies such as tinctures and decoctions, which are often employed to alleviate various health conditions.
